info.name: Amazon Neptune Management API
info.description:
Amazon Neptune Management API for creating, managing, and deleting Neptune DB clusters, instances, parameter groups, snapshots, and related infrastructure resources. This is an AWS Query API that uses HTTPS requests with Action and parameter query strings.

Contact Support:
 Name: Amazon Web Services

item.name: Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Clusters

query.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe.
Maximum number of records to include in the response (20-100).
Pagination token from a previous request.

request.description: Returns information about provisioned Neptune DB clusters. If a cluster identifier is supplied, details for that specific cluster are returned. Otherwise, details for up to 100 clusters are returned.

response.name: Successful response with DB cluster details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe.
Maximum number of records to include in the response (20-100).
Pagination token from a previous request.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The identifier of the DB cluster to describe.
Maximum number of records to include in the response (20-100).
Pagination token from a previous request.

item.name: Amazon Neptune Create a New Neptune DB Cluster

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier.
(Required) The database engine to use (neptune).
The version of the Neptune engine.
The port number on which the cluster accepts connections.
The name of a DB subnet group.
A list of VPC security group IDs.
A list of VPC security group IDs.
Whether the cluster is encrypted.
The KMS key identifier for an encrypted cluster.
The daily time range for automated backups (UTC).
The weekly time range for maintenance (UTC).
The number of days for which automated backups are retained (1-35).
Whether to enable deletion protection.
Whether to enable IAM database authentication.

request.description: Creates a new Amazon Neptune DB cluster. You can use the ReplicationSourceIdentifier parameter to create the DB cluster as a read replica of another DB cluster.

Whether to enable IAM database authentication.

item.name: ?Action=DeleteDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Delete a Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier for the cluster to be deleted.
Whether to skip creation of a final snapshot before deletion.
The identifier of the final snapshot to create before deletion.

request.description: Deletes a previously provisioned DB cluster. When you delete a DB cluster, all automated backups are deleted and cannot be recovered. Manual snapshots are not deleted.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier for the cluster to be deleted.
Whether to skip creation of a final snapshot before deletion.
The identifier of the final snapshot to create before deletion.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier for the cluster to be deleted.
Whether to skip creation of a final snapshot before deletion.
The identifier of the final snapshot to create before deletion.

item.name: ?Action=ModifyDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Modify a Neptune DB Cluster

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier.
The new cluster identifier when renaming.
The version of the Neptune engine to upgrade to.
The port number on which the cluster accepts connections.
The name of the cluster parameter group to use.
Whether to apply changes immediately or during the next maintenance window.

request.description: Modifies settings for a DB cluster. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying them and their new values.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier.
The new cluster identifier when renaming.
The version of the Neptune engine to upgrade to.
The port number on which the cluster accepts connections.
The name of the cluster parameter group to use.
Whether to apply changes immediately or during the next maintenance window.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB cluster identifier.
The new cluster identifier when renaming.
The version of the Neptune engine to upgrade to.
The port number on which the cluster accepts connections.
The name of the cluster parameter group to use.
Whether to apply changes immediately or during the next maintenance window.

item.name: ?Action=StartDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Start a Stopped Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to start.

request.description: Starts a stopped Neptune DB cluster. When you start a cluster, Neptune restores the cluster to the state it was in when it was stopped.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to start.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to start.

item.name: ?Action=StopDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Stop a Running Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to stop.

request.description: Stops a running Neptune DB cluster. While the cluster is stopped, you are charged only for storage. A stopped cluster automatically restarts after 7 days.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to stop.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to stop.

item.name: ?Action=FailoverDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Force a Failover for a Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster to force a failover for.
The name of the instance to promote to the primary instance.

request.description: Forces a failover for a DB cluster. A failover for a DB cluster promotes one of the read replicas in the cluster to be the new primary instance.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster to force a failover for.
The name of the instance to promote to the primary instance.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster to force a failover for.
The name of the instance to promote to the primary instance.

item.name: ?Action=AddRoleToDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Associate an IAM Role with a Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to associate the IAM role with.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to associate with the cluster.

request.description: Associates an Identity and Access Management (IAM) role with a Neptune DB cluster so that the cluster can access other AWS services on your behalf.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to associate the IAM role with.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to associate with the cluster.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to associate the IAM role with.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to associate with the cluster.

item.name: ?Action=RemoveRoleFromDBCluster
Amazon Neptune Disassociate an IAM Role from a Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to disassociate the role from.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to disassociate.

request.description: Disassociates an Identity and Access Management (IAM) role from a Neptune DB cluster.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to disassociate the role from.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to disassociate.

response.name: The specified DB cluster or role was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster to disassociate the role from.
(Required) The ARN of the IAM role to disassociate.

item.name: ?Action=CreateDBInstance
Amazon Neptune Create a New Neptune DB Instance

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier.
(Required) The compute and memory capacity of the instance.
(Required) The database engine to use (neptune).
(Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to add the instance to.
The Availability Zone for the instance.
Failover priority (0-15).

request.description: Creates a new DB instance in a Neptune DB cluster. The DB instance inherits the cluster's storage and high availability configuration.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier.
(Required) The compute and memory capacity of the instance.
(Required) The database engine to use (neptune).
(Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to add the instance to.
The Availability Zone for the instance.
Failover priority (0-15).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier.
(Required) The compute and memory capacity of the instance.
(Required) The database engine to use (neptune).
(Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to add the instance to.
The Availability Zone for the instance.
Failover priority (0-15).

response.name: A DB instance with the specified identifier already exists.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The DB instance identifier.
(Required) The compute and memory capacity of the instance.
(Required) The database engine to use (neptune).
(Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to add the instance to.
The Availability Zone for the instance.
Failover priority (0-15).

request.description: Modifies settings for a DB instance. You can change one or more database configuration parameters by specifying them and their new values.

item.name: ?Action=RebootDBInstance
Amazon Neptune Reboot a Neptune DB Instance

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB instance to reboot.
Whether to force a failover during the reboot.

request.description: Reboots a DB instance. When you reboot an instance, the instance is restarted. Rebooting results in a momentary outage.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB instance to reboot.
Whether to force a failover during the reboot.

response.name: The specified DB instance was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB instance to reboot.
Whether to force a failover during the reboot.

item.name: ?Action=CreateDBClusterSnapshot
Amazon Neptune Create a Snapshot of a Neptune DB Cluster

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to create a snapshot for.
(Required) The identifier for the DB cluster snapshot.

request.description: Creates a snapshot of a DB cluster. Manual snapshots can be used to restore a DB cluster to a specific state.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to create a snapshot for.
(Required) The identifier for the DB cluster snapshot.

response.name: The specified DB cluster was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the DB cluster to create a snapshot for.
(Required) The identifier for the DB cluster snapshot.

item.name: ?Action=DescribeDBClusterSnapshots
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Cluster Snapshots

query.description: The identifier of a specific snapshot to describe.
The identifier of the cluster to filter snapshots by.
Maximum number of records to include in the response.
Pagination token from a previous request.

request.description: Returns information about DB cluster snapshots. If a specific snapshot identifier is supplied, details for that snapshot are returned.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The identifier of a specific snapshot to describe.
The identifier of the cluster to filter snapshots by.
Maximum number of records to include in the response.
Pagination token from a previous request.

item.name: ?Action=DeleteDBClusterSnapshot
Amazon Neptune Delete a Neptune DB Cluster Snapshot

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster snapshot to delete.

request.description: Deletes a DB cluster snapshot. If the snapshot is being copied, the copy operation is terminated.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster snapshot to delete.

response.name: The specified snapshot was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The identifier of the cluster snapshot to delete.

item.name: ?Action=RestoreDBClusterFromSnapshot
Amazon Neptune Restore a Neptune DB Cluster from a Snapshot

urlencoded.description: (Required) The name of the new DB cluster.
(Required) The identifier of the snapshot to restore from.
(Required) The database engine to use.

request.description: Creates a new DB cluster from a DB snapshot or DB cluster snapshot. The new DB cluster is created from the source snapshot with a default configuration.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The name of the new DB cluster.
(Required) The identifier of the snapshot to restore from.
(Required) The database engine to use.

response.name: The specified snapshot was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

urlencoded.description: (Required) The name of the new DB cluster.
(Required) The identifier of the snapshot to restore from.
(Required) The database engine to use.

item.name: ?Action=CreateDBSubnetGroup
Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Subnet Group

query.description: (Required) The name for the DB subnet group.
(Required) The description for the DB subnet group.

request.description: Creates a new DB subnet group. DB subnet groups must contain at least one subnet in at least two Availability Zones in the AWS Region.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name for the DB subnet group.
(Required) The description for the DB subnet group.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name for the DB subnet group.
(Required) The description for the DB subnet group.

item.name: ?Action=DescribeDBSubnetGroups
Amazon Neptune Describe DB Subnet Groups

query.description: The name of a specific DB subnet group to describe.

request.description: Returns a list of DB subnet group descriptions. If a specific subnet group name is supplied, the description for that group is returned.

response.name: Successful response with subnet group details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The name of a specific DB subnet group to describe.

item.name: ?Action=CreateDBParameterGroup
Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Parameter Group

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name (e.g., neptune1, neptune1.2, neptune1.3).
(Required) The description for the DB parameter group.

request.description: Creates a new DB parameter group. A DB parameter group is initially created with the default parameters for the database engine used.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name (e.g., neptune1, neptune1.2, neptune1.3).
(Required) The description for the DB parameter group.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name (e.g., neptune1, neptune1.2, neptune1.3).
(Required) The description for the DB parameter group.

item.name: ?Action=CreateDBClusterParameterGroup
Amazon Neptune Create a New DB Cluster Parameter Group

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name.
(Required) The description for the DB cluster parameter group.

request.description: Creates a new DB cluster parameter group. Parameters in a DB cluster parameter group apply to all of the instances in a DB cluster.

response.name: DB cluster parameter group created successfully.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name.
(Required) The description for the DB cluster parameter group.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The name of the DB cluster parameter group.
(Required) The DB parameter group family name.
(Required) The description for the DB cluster parameter group.

query.description: The name of a specific DB parameter group to describe.

request.description: Returns a list of DB parameter group descriptions.

response.name: Successful response with parameter group details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The name of a specific DB parameter group to describe.

item.name: ?Action=DescribeDBClusterParameterGroups
Amazon Neptune Describe DB Cluster Parameter Groups

query.description: The name of a specific cluster parameter group to describe.

request.description: Returns a list of DB cluster parameter group descriptions.

response.name: Successful response with cluster parameter group details.

query.description: The name of a specific cluster parameter group to describe.

item.name: ?Action=CreateEventSubscription
Amazon Neptune Create a Neptune Event Notification Subscription

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the SNS topic to send notifications to.
The type of source that triggers notifications (db-instance, db-cluster, db-parameter-group, db-cluster-snapshot).

request.description: Creates an event notification subscription. This action requires an Amazon SNS topic ARN. Notifications are sent to the SNS topic when specified events occur.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the SNS topic to send notifications to.
The type of source that triggers notifications (db-instance, db-cluster, db-parameter-group, db-cluster-snapshot).

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the SNS topic to send notifications to.
The type of source that triggers notifications (db-instance, db-cluster, db-parameter-group, db-cluster-snapshot).

item.name: ?Action=DescribeEvents
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune Events

query.description: The identifier of the event source to filter events.
The event source type to filter events.
The number of minutes to retrieve events for.

request.description: Returns events related to DB instances, DB clusters, DB parameter groups, and DB cluster snapshots for the past 14 days.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The identifier of the event source to filter events.
The event source type to filter events.
The number of minutes to retrieve events for.

item.name: ?Action=AddTagsToResource
Amazon Neptune Add Tags to a Neptune Resource

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to add tags to.

request.description: Adds metadata tags to an Amazon Neptune resource. Tags are key-value pairs that you can use to categorize and manage your resources.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to add tags to.

response.name: The specified resource was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to add tags to.

item.name: ?Action=ListTagsForResource
Amazon Neptune List Tags for a Neptune Resource

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to list tags for.

request.description: Lists all tags on an Amazon Neptune resource.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to list tags for.

response.name: The specified resource was not found.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ARN of the resource to list tags for.

item.name: ?Action=DescribeDBEngineVersions
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune DB Engine Versions

query.description: The database engine to return versions for.
A specific engine version to return details for.
The parameter group family to filter versions by.

request.description: Returns a list of the available DB engine versions. The output shows the supported features and parameter group families for each version.

response.name: Successful response with engine version details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The database engine to return versions for.
A specific engine version to return details for.
The parameter group family to filter versions by.

item.name: ?Action=CreateGlobalCluster
Amazon Neptune Create a Neptune Global Database Cluster

query.description: (Required) The cluster identifier of the new global cluster.
The ARN of an existing Neptune cluster to use as the primary cluster.
Whether the global cluster has deletion protection enabled.

request.description: Creates a Neptune global database spread across multiple AWS Regions. The global database contains a single primary cluster with read-write capability.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The cluster identifier of the new global cluster.
The ARN of an existing Neptune cluster to use as the primary cluster.
Whether the global cluster has deletion protection enabled.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The cluster identifier of the new global cluster.
The ARN of an existing Neptune cluster to use as the primary cluster.
Whether the global cluster has deletion protection enabled.

item.name: ?Action=DescribeGlobalClusters
Amazon Neptune Describe Neptune Global Database Clusters

query.description: The identifier of the global cluster to describe.

request.description: Returns information about Neptune global database clusters.

response.name: Successful response with global cluster details.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: The identifier of the global cluster to describe.
